info.title: beste.schule
info.description:
Welcome to the beste.schule API. This reference documents every endpoint, its
parameters and its responses.

## Authentication & base paths

Every route below exists under **two** prefixes that share the same controllers
but authenticate differently:

| Prefix | Audience | Authentication |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **`/api`** | Third-party / external integrations | **OAuth 2.0** access token sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. |
| **`/web`** | Our own first-party JavaScript app on the same domain | **Session cookie** (plus CSRF token). Not meant for external clients. |

**This documentation describes the `/api` (OAuth) variant.** The `/web` variant
is identical in shape but is reserved for the in-browser application and relies
on the session of the logged-in user.

## Roles

Access is governed by a strict role hierarchy
(`admin` ⊃ `mod` ⊃ `management` ⊃ `teacher` ⊃ `guardian` ⊃ `student`). A higher
role always satisfies the requirements of a lower one. **Each operation carries a
role badge** showing the minimum role required to call it, e.g.
`🔒 Guardian+ Required`. The trailing `+` means "this role and any higher one"
(so `Teacher+` is also open to management, mods and admins); `🔓 Any role` means
every authenticated role may call it.

## Query parameters

Index endpoints accept the following query parameters (documented per endpoint):

- **`filter[...]`** – filter the result set, e.g. `?filter[student]=12,34`. The
  global entity filters `group`, `student`, `guardian`, `teacher`, `subject`,
  `room`, `interval`, `year` and `role` are accepted on every endpoint.
- **`sort`** – sort by one or more fields; prefix with `-` for descending order.
- **`include`** – eager-load related resources.
- **`append`** – append computed attributes to the returned resource(s).

Multiple values are comma-separated and can be combined freely.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter the result set, e.g. `?filter[student]=123,456`. The properties below list the filters this endpoint accepts; the global entity filters are accepted everywhere.

range.description: Filter by `range`. Comma-separate for multiple values.

date.description: Filter by `date`. Comma-separate for multiple values.

present.description: Filter by `present`. Comma-separate for multiple values.

guardian.description: Global entity filter: restrict to this guardian. Accepted on every endpoint. Comma-separate for multiple values.

subject.description: Global entity filter: restrict to this subject. Accepted on every endpoint. Comma-separate for multiple values.

guardian_id.description: Filter by `guardian_id`. Comma-separate for multiple values.

group.description: Global entity filter: restrict to this group. Accepted on every endpoint. Comma-separate for multiple values.

student.description: Global entity filter: restrict to this student. Accepted on every endpoint. Comma-separate for multiple values.

teacher.description: Global entity filter: restrict to this teacher. Accepted on every endpoint. Comma-separate for multiple values.

room.description: Global entity filter: restrict to this room. Accepted on every endpoint. Comma-separate for multiple values.

interval.description: Global entity filter: restrict to this interval. Accepted on every endpoint. Comma-separate for multiple values.

year.description: Global entity filter: restrict to this year. Accepted on every endpoint. Comma-separate for multiple values.

role.description: Global entity filter: restrict to users of this role. Accepted on every endpoint.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort the result set. Prefix a field with `-` for descending order. Multiple fields may be comma-separated. Sortable fields: `id`, `from`, `to`.

get.description:
How often was each student present or absent per time slot, where a slot is defined
as a combination of ISO weekday (1 = Monday … 7 = Sunday) and lesson period number
(`lesson_nr`), broken down into excused and unexcused.
The result can be used to calculate unexcused absences by subtracting
`not_present_with_absence_count` from `not_present_count`.
get.summary: Journal Lesson Student Statistics: By time slot (Durchsitzansicht)

get.description:
Returns all lessons within the given date range that are incomplete.
A lesson is considered incomplete if either:
  (a) A substitution plan entry exists but no journal lesson was created ("no_journal"), or
  (b) A journal lesson exists but it is missing notes, students, or has a non-"hold" status.

Within case (b), a journal lesson that is neither held nor canceled while a matching
canceled substitution plan entry exists is reported as "not_canceled": it must be
confirmed as canceled rather than marked as held.

Management users see all data school-wide. Teachers must supply filter[group] or filter[student].

message.example: The filter[range_from] and filter[range_to] fields are required.
The filter[group], filter[student] or filter[teacher] field is required.

timetables whose validity period overlaps that date range.

get.description:
Return the timetable valid for the user's current year (computed from the
year's date range; the most recently valid one wins).

get.description:
Clear the two-factor secret of the authenticated user. Mods may disable
two-factor for another user by passing that user's `id`.

Generate and store a new two-factor secret for the authenticated user and
return it so the client can render the setup QR code.

Clear the two-factor secret of the authenticated user. Mods may disable
two-factor for another user by passing that user's `id`.

post.description:
Validate a one-time password against the user's secret and, on success,
mark two-factor as verified. Aborts with 420 if no secret has been set up.

get.description:
Generate a random password for the given user, store it and clear their
two-factor secret, then return the plain password once (mod action).

delete.description:
Called on your own account, this deletes it entirely and logs you out.
Called on another user (mods), it only removes them from the current
school — mods cannot fully delete other users.

get.description:
Return the authenticated user with all related data (person records,
school, config, favorites, devices).

Return the authenticated user with all related data (person records,
school, config, favorites, devices).

delete.description:
Called on your own account, this deletes it entirely and logs you out.
Called on another user (mods), it only removes them from the current
school — mods cannot fully delete other users.

post.description:
Link the authenticated account to a school/person via an invitation
`token`. Depending on the token type a guardian record may be created
(then `guardian_forename`/`guardian_name` are required).

put.description:
Switch the authenticated user's active school. For web sessions the user
is re-logged in to refresh the session (two-factor state is preserved).

get.description: Revoke the current OAuth access token.
Revoke the current OAuth access token.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort the result set. Prefix a field with `-` for descending order. Multiple fields may be comma-separated. Sortable fields: `from`.

post.description: Restore a soft-deleted year.

Set the active year for the authenticated user. Pass `id: null` to clear
the manual selection — the current year is then determined automatically
from today's date, merging overlapping years into a single current year.

http.description: OAuth 2.0 access token. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.
